description: On a tree or another plant that may later bear fruit, a flower can be called 'phirali'. The noun points to the blossom itself. 'peloru' (flower) is the general word, while 'sheloa' (bloom) presents flowering as an event.
articulatory_notes: A soft stream of unvoiced air passes between nearly closed lips for 'ph' before high-front 'i'. The tongue taps or briefly trills into stressed open 'ra', then settles at the alveolar ridge for lateral 'li'.
usage_notes: While attached, a counted blossom can take living classifier 'lipha'. Once cut and handled as an object, it can take 'themo'; either classifier remains optional.

semantic_domains.nature: a flower, especially one associated with later fruit
